reference: beams pointing at increasing scan angles. In a previous patent , simultaneous multiple beams are generated using OFDM multiple antennas being fed at different points along a trans- mission line providing delay or phase differences between successive antennas, but the use of such a physical delay source limits its capability and ﬂexibility. On the other hand, OFDM signals with various delays can be produced with CC by ﬂexibly exploiting a programmable waveform gener- ator (WG). In the CC-OFDM MIMO radar, a novel technique can be used further to mitigate beam broadening for large scanning angles. In a way, the strategy of CC-OFDM MIMO radar can be viewed as a realization of hybrid between phased array and MIMO techniques , .

The echo signals caused by two or more targets at different ranges (i.e., one target is near to the radar, and the other is far) could cause range ambiguity. reference: guidance for understanding, planning, and implementing effective and efficient IT management principles . By adopting COBIT 2019, organizations can improve their IT performance, reduce risks, and ensure that IT is used to support the achievement of business objectives optimally. Domain DSS01 in COBIT 2019, titled "Define and Manage Service Levels," addresses the management and establishment of service levels within the scope of an organization's IT . Within DSS01, organizations are required to identify specific service requirements, including performance and reliability criteria, and manage user expectations regarding IT services. Additionally, this domain also covers the measurement and monitoring of service performance, as well as the adjustment processes necessary to ensure that the services provided remain in line with established standards. Generally, log-based anomaly detection can be performed using either point, collective, or contextual approaches. In the log-based anomaly detection literature, most of the existing studies propose either a contextual or collective approach, where the decision is taken by examining a sequence of log events. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. Therefore, several studies explored utilising a point-based approach to detect anomalous events on logs data, such as in operating systems , , drone devices , and distribution systems , . reference: Existing anomaly detection modelling techniques can be conducted either in a point, contextual, or collective setting. Point anomaly detection characterises each log entry, whether an event is an abnormality or a normal event. For instance, if a user types the wrong password when trying to gain access to an operating system prompt, the generated log message can be considered as an anomaly , , . As for contextual anomaly, the detection involves analysing some contextually related attributes to determine whether a log entry is an anomaly. The unfiltered dataset is a collection of log messages in its original condition gathered from the two sources without performing unique filtering. Therefore, there are several duplicate log messages both in training and testing sets. This is aligned with the real case scenario, where a drone tends to generate the same log message when the event is still happening. It indicates that the current event has yet to be changed or solved (if it is an issue). On the other hand, the filtered dataset is a collection of log messages after a unique filtering process. CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. An extensive experiment proved that the proposed approach is better than the previous work baselines while being inferior to our baseline scenario based on the accuracy and F1

user_input: FOR LONG-RANGE SURVEILLANCE A. REQUIREMENTS A. REQUIREMENTS To elucidate the novel concept and detailed implementation of the CC-OFDM MIMO radar, we will take a surveillance radar design as an example for analyses in the subsequent sections. The CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform designed herein is based on the requirement of surveillance radars used in coastal areas, where the target of interest are ships and small aircrafts in the area within 180 km from the radar site. The receive scheduling is also related to the time scheduling of the correlation between symbols, which is an important stage in the beam and range processing. The transmit and receive time schedule of CC-OFDM radar is described in Fig. 4. The receive scheduling is very important, especially in a multiple target scheme. The echo signals caused by two or more targets at different ranges (i.e., one target is near to the radar, and the other is far) could cause range ambiguity. reference: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) , is a variation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) that has been successfully applied to various Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. LSTM has the ability to capture long-range dependencies with sequences making it suitable for solving sequence labeling problems , , , such as Named Entity Recognition tagging (POS-Tag) and Neural (NER), Part-of-Speech combining (NMT). Study Machine Translation Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) model with Conditional Random Field (CRF) to solve NER and POS-Tag Task. BiLSTM encodes word or character vectors into features, while CRF is a classifier to predict the target labels. Previous research has explored enhancing the feature extractor by integrating serves attention mechanisms with existing models or introducing alternative extractors such as Convolutional Neural Networks

user_input: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

However, the proposed framework has not explained the preparation phase in detail. Therefore, another framework consisting of four investigative phases with a more comprehensive analysis is proposed by Al-Dhaqm et al. . user_input: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. reference: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

CONCLUSION AND FUTURE WORK In this paper, we demonstrate how to train a log-based anomaly detection model that can prioritise the prediction of higher severity anomalies on drone flight logs while increasing the prediction confidence score at the same time. Considering the nature of the dataset, where samples that belong to different severity levels share common features, a multitask vector label representation along with severity-oriented decoding is proposed. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. user_input: on critical findings. The proposed embedding, DroneSBERT, improved the trustworthiness of the model, as evidenced by the word’s importance report. Despite the promising results, the model’s generality remains untested as the training dataset contains only log messages acquired from 82 drone models, mostly from DJI. This is one of the main challenges in drone forensics research, where human-readable flight log datasets from other drone models are unavailable. Tested on seven models, it outperformed traditional methods, achieving high fitness and precision, especially in complex scenarios. GRAPH DATABASE Graph database models are defined as the data structure in which schema and instances are modeled as graphs and graph-oriented operations are performed on graphs. Instead of relying on resource-intensive transfer learning models, our approach optimizes model efficiency, making it more com- putationally friendly or resource-constrained applications. This contribution aims to streamline the implementation of emotion recognition systems without compromising perfor- mance. The proposed research is based on a simplified deep learn- ing architecture and channel selection approach that leads towards closer and resource-constrained real-time implemen- tation. In addition, research for cross-subject validation is more likely to be implemented widely than dependent subject validation. Cross-subject validation has a better generaliza- tion model than dependent-subject validation. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. Models pretrained on these datasets have optimized weights for recognizing common features, mitigating issues associated with small datasets. Transfer learning and fine-tuning allow models to adapt to new datasets, significantly reducing training time. Studies utilizing pretrained models include for detect- ing polyps with YOLOv4, for Crohn’s disease lesions, for pre-colorectal cancer abnormalities, for bladder cancer lesions, and for cervical lesions. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
